source.title: The functional role of cross-frequency coupling

confidence_rationale: Major review directly addressing the functional role of theta-phase/gamma-amplitude coupling across human cortex, hippocampus, and prefrontal circuits. Provides both the mechanistic account (slow oscillation phase organises fast-oscillation bursts) and the cognitive function (enabling communication across spatial and temporal scales between hierarchically distinct systems). The packaging-for-higher-level-read-out mechanism is explicit and maps cleanly to MECH-089's ThetaBuffer. Slightly higher confidence than Lisman & Jensen (2013) for MECH-089 because this review covers the general cortical case, not just rodent hippocampal spatial coding, reducing species-transfer risk.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Theta-phase to gamma-amplitude coupling (theta-gamma PAC) is observed across the human hippocampus, entorhinal cortex, and prefrontal cortex during memory encoding, working memory, and attentional tasks. The slow oscillation (theta) phase acts as a temporal frame: gamma bursts at preferred theta phases carry task-relevant information, allowing slower hierarchical systems to read out fast local computations in batches. CFC thereby enables communication across circuits operating at incompatible timescales and constitutes a general neural mechanism for hierarchical integration.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-089: CFC is the biological substrate of the REE ThetaBuffer. E1 generates fast sensory prediction-error signals at gamma-equivalent rates; CFC bundles these into theta-phase-aligned packets accessible to E3, which operates at a slower deliberation rate. E3 never directly samples raw E1 gamma-rate output — it reads theta-cycle-packaged summaries. The human prefrontal and hippocampal CFC evidence in this review extends the Lisman & Jensen rodent spatial-coding result to the domain most relevant to REE's planning and moral-attribution hierarchy.
mapping.mapping_caveat: CFC is most reliably measured during memory encoding and sustained attention; whether the same packaging mechanism operates during rapid online prediction-error integration (E1's function) is inferential. The review describes CFC as a communication mechanism but does not directly demonstrate that downstream (slow) systems are computationally blind to fast-oscillation content outside of theta windows — the 'E3 never sees raw E1' claim is stronger than the source directly establishes.
mapping.source_context: Review of human intracranial EEG, scalp EEG, and rodent LFP; hippocampus, entorhinal cortex, prefrontal cortex, and motor cortex; memory encoding, working memory maintenance, and attentional task paradigms.

confidence_components.notes: Very high source quality — canonical CFC review with broad empirical coverage across species and task domains; mapping to ThetaBuffer is structurally faithful; lower transfer risk than Lisman & Jensen because human prefrontal CFC is directly reviewed.
